title: Who Was I Before the Baby?
subtitle: A journal practice for recovering the pre-mother version of yourself
excerpt: A journal practice for exploring who you were before you became a mother: what you cared about, what you did with time, what you wanted. Not as a way of going back, but as a way of knowing what parts of that person to carry forward.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you feel like you have lost yourself in motherhood, or when you want to reconnect with the person who existed before the role took over.

content.text: A twenty-minute journal practice for recovering the pre-mother version of yourself. Not because you want to go back, but because knowing who you were before you had children shows you what parts of that person are still present and what parts you might want to bring back into the foreground.
Describe a typical Saturday when you were in your twenties, before children.
Not an ideal Saturday. A typical one. What time you got up, what you did, who you were with, what you cared about that day.
What did you want from life then that you still want now?
Write the specific things, not the vague ones. The wanting to make things. The wanting to travel somewhere. The wanting to write or read or have time alone.
What parts of the pre-children version of you are still present, even if quieter?
The curiosity, the humour, the ambition, the particular thing you cared about. Name what has not been extinguished, only compressed.
If you were to bring one aspect of that person back into your current life, what would it be?
Make it specific and make it possible in the life you have now. Not a fantasy. An actual thing that could be real.
